changes_made: Renamed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled to k8s_service_mtls_enabled for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to secure communication channels and encryption in transit. The naming mostly follows conventions, but a minor adjustment was made for consistency. The functions are descriptive and clearly indicate the security checks being performed.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of resource names, such as 'service' instead of 'service_mesh' to align with existing patterns.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | security attribute binding [withdrawn
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(18) relates to enforcing secure information flow through security attributes like encryption and secure transport. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure communication channels and encryption in transit. This involves enabling TLS for Ingress resources, ensuring mutual TLS (mTLS) in service meshes, and securing API server and etcd communications with TLS.
